attacking_style: Breakaway specialist who positions in early moves, then conserves energy throughout the stage to unleash a calculated sprint finish. Employs psychological gamesmanship in breaks, disrupting cooperation before final kilometers.
team_tactics: Uses domestiques (e.g., CONTI) to force accelerations and bleed energy from breakaway companions. Minimal team reliance in finals—relies on individual tactics and energy conservation rather than lead-out trains.
terrain_preference: Excels on uphill sprint finishes and classics-type steep gradients where climbing ability meets positioning skills. Strong in varied terrain (Strade Bianche chase groups, rolling stages) but not pure mountain stages.
vulnerability: Struggles to bridge gaps when isolated against stronger time trialists or pure climbers. Sprint positioning irregularities (2021 disqualification). Cannot respond when caught out by early powerful attacks (VAN AVERMAET 2018).
key_patterns: Breakaway energy conservation: refuses collaboration with companions, saving watts for final sprint (4 observations)
Psychological warfare in breaks: creates dissent and argues with companions to disrupt cooperation (3 observations)
Uphill sprint finisher: consistently places top-3 on steep gradient finales mixing power and positioning
Grand Tour stage hunting: targets breakaway stages in Vuelta/Giro rather than GC support role
Sprint calculation over pure power: times efforts perfectly, holding narrow margins (2-second wins, line catches)
form_trajectory: positive: 2023 Vuelta stage win after decade drought demonstrates sustained tactical sharpness and sprint finishing at age 37, plus Strade Bianche top-5 chase suggests maintained climbing form
built_from: 2023 La Vuelta Ciclista a Espana Stage 15
